response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software engineer with over 20 years of career history, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application-layer software development. The Principal Performance Architect role requires deep expertise in SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload analysis — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest adjacent experience is a brief stint writing low-latency D-language services handling HTTP traffic (Sociomantic) and some recent AI-agent tooling work, neither of which constitutes hardware-software co-design or silicon-level performance analysis. He does not meet the technical profile for this role.
strengths: 20+ years of professional software engineering experience across diverse stacks and domains, satisfying the tenure threshold for a BS-level candidate
Demonstrated performance-consciousness in low-latency, high-throughput systems (async I/O, fibers, caching at Sociomantic)
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling, showing awareness of the current AI landscape
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience of any kind — the primary technical requirement for the role is entirely absent
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning workload optimization background, whether in training or inference
Career trajectory is firmly in application-layer and web engineering with no hardware-software co-design, silicon prototyping, or computer architecture work

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No deep-learning or AI accelerator workload expertise
Long tenure and systems-level performance work
